summary: A git push performed by the sync script failed because the CI bot account lacked permission to push to the repository, causing the job to exit with an error.
root_cause: git push failed with a 403 permission error: the account 'nextjs-bot' was denied permission to push to vercel/next.js, causing the git command to exit with code 128.

evidence.quote: remote: Permission to vercel/next.js.git denied to nextjs-bot.
evidence.reason: Explicit permission denial showing the actor (nextjs-bot) is not allowed to access the repository.

evidence.quote: fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js/': The requested URL returned error: 403
evidence.reason: Git reports an HTTP 403 response when trying to access the repository, indicating an authorization issue.

evidence.quote: Error: Command failed with exit code 128 (Unknown system error -128): git push origin update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422
evidence.reason: The failing command and non-zero exit code show the push operation failed and aborted the job.
Stack trace points to the sync script that attempted the git push, identifying where the failing operation was invoked.
CI job failed as a result of the command error, confirming this caused job failure.

suggested_fix: Ensure the CI uses credentials with push access: grant the nextjs-bot push permission to vercel/next.js or update the job to use a personal access token or deploy key with appropriate repo write scopes; verify the git remote/auth configuration used by scripts/sync-react.js before running git push.
